summary.intent: User completed a large refactoring (splitting monolithic Rust files into modules, adding `pub` visibility) and wanted verification that no behavioral changes were introduced.
summary.outcome: Two real bugs were found and partially fixed: (1) `runner.rs` was deleted after confirming `runner/mod.rs` + submodules were complete replacements; (2) unresolved merge conflict markers in `cron_tool.rs` were being resolved when the session was interrupted, leaving `cron_tool.rs` in a partially-fixed state.

repo: File size limit is 1,500 lines enforced by `scripts/check-file-size.sh` with an allowlist; the refactoring goal was to empty the allowlist entirely.
CUDA crates require a CUDA toolkit and CMake/llama.cpp build deps not present on macOS — exclude them when running local tests.
The workspace uses a Rust nightly toolchain.
Clippy results were cached, masking a real compile error in the agents crate caused by the `runner.rs` / `runner/mod.rs` coexistence conflict.

code.finding: Original 9,414-line file was never deleted after the module split into runner/mod.rs + submodules, causing a Rust module conflict. File was deleted as the fix.

Unresolved git merge conflict markers starting at line 866; the <<<<<<< HEAD side (mod tests;) is correct since cron_tool/tests.rs already contains all 25 test functions. The =======...>>>>>>> block is dead duplicate code.

workflow: Clippy caching can mask compile errors — always do a clean check (`cargo check`) when verifying structural refactoring, not just clippy.
After a module split, explicitly verify the original file was deleted; Rust will silently prefer one over the other depending on edition, hiding the conflict from normal builds.
Running `cargo check` per-crate is more reliable than workspace-level checks for catching module resolution errors in a specific crate.

friction: Clippy passed (from cache) despite the agents crate not actually compiling, giving a false green signal early in verification.
The file size check script also gave a false pass because the allowlist was emptied but the oversized files (`runner.rs`, `cron_tool.rs`) were never re-checked against the limit.
CUDA/llama.cpp build failures on macOS required multiple attempts to find the right `cargo test` invocation that excluded incompatible crates.
Resolving the large merge conflict block in `cron_tool.rs` required multiple Read/Edit iterations due to file size and offset navigation.
open_items: cron_tool.rs conflict resolution was interrupted mid-fix — the file still contains partial merge conflict content and needs the remaining `=======` through `>>>>>>> origin/main` block removed (lines ~868–2075).
After fixing cron_tool.rs, a full `cargo check` and clippy run on the entire workspace should be done to confirm both issues are fully resolved.
Re-run the file size check script after fixes to confirm all files are under 1,500 lines with an empty allowlist.
